summary: Completed backend + frontend validation for staff authorization controls, inactive crew-link lifecycle behavior, owner queue pagination (>8), heatmap legend visibility, owner calibration flow, and Sarver-branded jobs/settings/owner/analytics pages.
action_items: No blocking regressions found for requested updates.
Optional maintainability: split /app/backend/server.py into focused modules to reduce regression risk.
Google Drive live OAuth remains unconfigured in this environment; only status/loading was validated.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py is very large (>700 lines) and should be modularized (auth, jobs, submissions, reviews, analytics, exports, integrations).
test_credentials: Used Production Manager and Owner credentials from /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST staff account(s), TEST crew link(s), and TEST submissions via APIs to validate status toggles and owner queue pagination data volume.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 4 generated owner-queue test data so owner pagination now shows Page 1 of 2 with 8 visible items on first page. One crew link was deactivated intentionally during UI verification and is now visible under inactive links. No mocked APIs used.
rca of the issue: No functional blocker reproduced. A transient UI false-negative occurred when owner pagination was checked before async queue fetch completed; after explicit wait, pagination controls and page transitions worked as expected.
